executive_summary: Global destabilisation risk remains moderate as the contrast between perceived regional panic and actual physical infrastructure change in East Asia indicates highly narrative driven volatility. While Middle East energy fluctuations are escalating, the current buffer of global fuel reserves maintains systemic baseline stability. Consequently, supply chain vulnerabilities and elevated artificial intelligence market indices require targeted monitoring to prevent localised disruptions from cascading.
correlations: The surge in Middle East energy volatility directly compounds the global supply chain stress score as transport networks price in heightened risk.
